A.rationale: Nails registry+decorator+auto-discovery decoupling and inline decoupling test, but garbles the upper/reverse order proof and ships a corrupted smoke command (stray CJK char in code).

B.rationale: Tightest, signal-dense plan; correct auto-discovery decoupling, catches the order false-positive with an asymmetric plugin, clean risk table, thread-safety, decoupling acceptance test.

C.rationale: Most complete: rigorous commute analysis merging order+decoupling test, git-diff acceptance check, alternatives rejecting explicit-list as violating constraint; long with illustrative code but stayed a plan.

notes: All three nail registry+@register+pkgutil auto-discovery decoupling. A loses on a corrupted smoke command and muddled order proof. B and C both correct and complete; B edges it on discipline (tighter, signal-dense) while C is longer with inline code.
